skill.name: avalonia-zafiro-development
skill.description: Mandatory skills, conventions, and behavioral rules for Avalonia UI development using the Zafiro toolkit.
skill.summary: Development conventions and rules for Avalonia UI with Zafiro toolkit emphasizing functional-reactive MVVM patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. This skill contains only markdown documentation for Avalonia UI development conventions. The flagged patterns (backtick execution, weak crypto, system reconnaissance) are triggered by markdown syntax and development terminology in documentation context, not actual executable code. No scripts, network calls, file system access, or environment variable usage present.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Commands Detection (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Backtick characters in markdown files are used for code formatting, not shell execution. The skill contains no executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Keywords in documentation context do not represent actual cryptographic code. This is a documentation-only skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: System Reconnaissance Detection (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Development terminology like 'Search First' in documentation is not actual system reconnaissance. Documentation-only skill with no executable components.

content.value_statement: This skill enforces mandatory conventions for Avalonia UI development with Zafiro toolkit, ensuring maintainable functional-reactive MVVM code that follows industry best practices.
actual_capabilities: Enforce functional-reactive MVVM architecture patterns using DynamicData and ReactiveUI
Provide naming and coding standards for consistent C# development
Guide validation patterns using Zafiro extensions and Result types
Offer shortcut references for common Rx and DynamicData operations
Define cross-platform UI development rules for Avalonia
Prevent common anti-patterns in reactive collection handling
limitations: Does not execute code or provide runtime functionality
Requires existing knowledge of C# and MVVM patterns
Does not include Zafiro library implementation details
Focuses on conventions rather than complete implementation guides

use_cases.title: New Avalonia Project Setup
use_cases.description: Apply Zafiro conventions when starting a new Avalonia UI project to establish proper architecture from the beginning.
use_cases.target_user: Developers new to Avalonia Zafiro development

use_cases.title: Code Review and Refactoring
use_cases.description: Use the skill to review existing code against Zafiro patterns and identify areas needing refactoring.

use_cases.title: Learning Reactive Patterns
use_cases.description: Reference the shortcuts and patterns when implementing DynamicData pipelines or reactive commands.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Zafiro Conventions
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the Avalonia Zafiro development conventions to review and improve this code. Focus on MVVM purity, DynamicData usage, and Zafiro helper methods.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have C# code that needs to follow Zafiro patterns

prompt_templates.title: Fix Reactive Anti-Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Identify and fix reactive anti-patterns in this code using Zafiro best practices. Look for ad-hoc SourceList creation, logic in Subscribe, and improper operator usage.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement validation using Zafiro ValidationRule and extensions. Show the proper pattern for validating dynamic collections.
prompt_templates.scenario: When adding form or data validation

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Collection Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply RefreshableCollection pattern and DynamicData operators to optimize collection handling in this code.
prompt_templates.scenario: When working with dynamic lists and collections

output_examples.input: Write a ViewModel for a list of items that can be filtered and refreshed.
output_examples.output: A ViewModel using RefreshableCollection with DynamicData Connect/Filter/Transform operators, proper DisposeWith lifecycle management, and Zafiro validation rules.

output_examples.input: Convert this error handling to use Result types.
output_examples.output: Code refactored to use CSharpFunctionalExtensions Result type with proper Success/Failure handling pipelines instead of exceptions for flow control.

output_examples.input: Show the Zafiro shortcut for Replay(1).RefCount().
output_examples.output: Use ReplayLastActive() extension method from Zafiro.Reactive.ObservableMixin.

best_practices: Prefer DynamicData operators over plain Rx operators when working with collections
Use Result types from CSharpFunctionalExtensions for error handling instead of exceptions
Keep ViewModels strictly Avalonia-independent for cross-platform compatibility
anti_patterns: Creating SourceList or SourceCache on the fly for local problems instead of using existing helpers
Placing business logic inside Subscribe blocks instead of in the reactive pipeline
Using plain System.Reactive operators when DynamicData equivalents exist

faq.question: What is Zafiro in Avalonia development?
faq.answer: Zafiro is a toolkit that provides abstractions, helpers, and extension methods for Avalonia UI development, focusing on reactive patterns and clean architecture.

faq.question: Why use DynamicData over plain Rx?
faq.answer: DynamicData provides specialized operators for collection management (Connect, Filter, Transform, Sort) that are more efficient and idiomatic for UI binding scenarios.

faq.question: What does pure MVVM mean in this context?
faq.answer: ViewModels must not reference Avalonia types directly, ensuring they remain testable and platform-independent.

faq.question: When should I use Result types?
faq.answer: Use Result types for expected error paths and flow control. Reserve exceptions for truly exceptional, unrecoverable situations.

faq.question: What is RefreshableCollection?
faq.answer: A Zafiro pattern that manages refreshable lists with automatic loading, efficient updates via EditDiff, and ReadOnlyObservableCollection output for UI binding.

faq.question: How do I handle errors in reactive pipelines?
faq.answer: Use Zafiro's HandleErrorsWith extension to pipe errors directly to the UI notification service instead of manual Subscribe error handling.
